subject: Issue-ID: AAI-1097 SSL_BASIC getting 403 error
commit_message:
Issue-ID: AAI-1097 SSL_BASIC getting 403 error

Change-Id: I3c09aa11870c4c104deb2d08cdcab3ee062d3235
Signed-off-by: Ashoka M G <ashok.g@arris.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/aai-sparky-be-master-verify-java/175/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/aai-sparky-be-master-verify-java/175

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

This is assigned to a medium priority bug in Jira - is this intended to be included with Beijing?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Can you reproduce this on wind river lab? Our local testing did not give us a 403 error when we called AAI and we use SSL_BASIC auth.

What error logs did you see on AAI?
Also please provide the exact steps to reproduce, in the Jira which will be helpful

Patch Set 1:

Since I don't have access to wind river lab, I have set up AAI locally by setting up docker by referring https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/How+to+Docker+setup+on+Single+VM. 
Steps to reproduce issue : 
Make sure following configuration present in sparky-core-sync.xml:
       <property name="restAuthenticationMode" value="SSL_BASIC" />
       <property name="basicAuthUserName" value="AAI" />
       <property name="basicAuthPassword" value="AAI" />
Now if we run all the dockers without my fix we can see errors similar to following in /opt/aai/logroot/AAI-UI/error.log. 
https://aai.api.simpledemo.onap.org:8443/aai/v13/search/nodes-query?search-node-type=service-instance&filter=service-instance-id:EXISTS resulted in http response: 403 Forbidden|
With my fix if we rebuild sparky-be and run the docker then we see logs like this: 
https://aai.api.simpledemo.onap.org:8443/aai/v13/search/nodes-query?search-node-type=service-instance&filter=service-instance-id:EXISTS resulted in http response: 200 OK|.

Patch Set 1:

@Arul, how is this working in the other envs?  Using different settings?

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

> @Arul, the code I have commented is the place where headers getting
 > initialized for first time and the method is used only locally.
 > However I see your point to put defensive code but I think that
 > should be there in RestClient.getClientBuilder which is common
 > code. This is the place where Authorization header is getting added
 > again. But this is different project and latest version is
 > different than referred version hence thought of fixing the issue
 > in the first place itself.
 > May be we can have the defensive code in https://github.com/onap/aai-rest-client/blob/master/src/main/java/org/onap/aai/restclient/client/RestClient.java
 > -> Line #614 as follows:
 > if (clientBuilder.getAuthenticationMode() == RestAuthenticationMode.SSL_BASIC
 > && headers.get(Headers.AUTHORIZATION) == null) {
 > builder = builder.header(Headers.AUTHORIZATION,
 > clientBuilder.getBasicAuthenticationCredentials());
 > }
 > 
 > Meanwhile I have cleaned up unused imports but unable to push the
 > code using "git push origin HEAD:refs/for/master". Please suggest
 > if there is any other way .

Hi @ashok to push changes to existing review. Make your changes in the same repo, from where you pushed this review. And perform a git commit with the exact message so that gerrit review -s will add it to the this change id insteading of creating a new one.

Patch Set 1:

@Arul Since fixing the common library RestClient itself to avoid duplicate header will solve the issue even if called from multiple places, have done the change there itself and will abandon this commit. Kindly check the other commit : https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/46179/

Abandoned

Kindly check the other commit : https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/46179/
